leagueBriefing: Marcus Ericsson ended a 13-year pole drought in spectacular fashion, dominating the inaugural qualifying session on the streets of Arlington.

games.headline: Ericsson ends 13-year drought with dominant pole at inaugural Arlington GP
games.brief: Marcus Ericsson delivered a masterclass in the first-ever qualifying session for the Grand Prix of Arlington, securing his first career IndyCar pole and his first top starting spot in any series since 2013. In a new 'Fast Six' format featuring single-car, single-lap runs, Ericsson went out first and set a blistering pace that heavy hitters like Alex Palou and Pato O'Ward simply couldn't match. The session proved to be a survival of the fittest on the new 2.73-mile street circuit, with several championship contenders finding the walls while Ericsson remained clinical.
bullets: Marcus Ericsson's lap of 1m34.3562s secured his first pole in 117 career IndyCar starts.
Reigning champion Alex Palou qualified second, trailing Ericsson by a significant 0.4618s margin.
Scott McLaughlin, a pre-qualifying favorite, will start from the back of the pack after a heavy crash in the second group session.
The session debuted a new knockout qualifying format where the 'Fast Six' competed via individual single-lap flyers.
Andretti Global placed two cars in the top four, with Will Power joining pole-sitter Ericsson on the second row.
games.context: This pole marks a major milestone for Ericsson as he looks to establish himself as a consistent qualifying threat alongside his proven race-winning pedigree.
